report_name: Russian APT Gamaredon Enhances Malware and Evasion Techniques in Ukraine War

threat_actors.name: Gamaredon
threat_actors.description: A Russian-backed APT group that has upgraded its TTPs in its cyber espionage campaign against Ukraine. The group uses high-volume spear-phishing, abuses legitimate cloud services like Cloudflare for C2, and provides initial access for other Russian APTs like Turla.
threat_actors.goals: Espionage and data exfiltration from Ukrainian government and military targets.

threat_actors.name: Turla
threat_actors.description: A high-profile Russian APT group observed collaborating with Gamaredon. Turla receives initial access from Gamaredon and then deploys its own sophisticated Kazuar malware framework for post-exploitation activities.
threat_actors.goals: Espionage and data exfiltration from Ukrainian government and military targets.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: Gamaredon employed spear-phishing emails containing malicious LNK or HTA files within archive files to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: Users were targeted with malicious LNK or HTA files that required execution to deliver the initial payload.

attack_patterns.name: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Gamaredon used PowerShell scripts as initial droppers to download next-stage malware.

attack_patterns.name: Visual Basic
attack_patterns.description: Gamaredon used VBS scripts as initial droppers to download next-stage malware.

attack_patterns.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor achieved persistence by exploiting CVE-2025-8088 to place downloader payloads in the Windows Startup folder.

attack_patterns.name: Bidirectional Communication
attack_patterns.description: Gamaredon used legitimate cloud services like Cloudflare for C2, masking malicious traffic within legitimate web traffic to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The PteroSand and Kazuar backdoors used web protocols for command and control communications.

malware.name: PteroSand
malware.description: A backdoor used by Gamaredon, downloaded by initial PowerShell or VBS droppers.

malware.name: Kazuar
malware.description: A sophisticated malware framework deployed by the Turla group after gaining access to a compromised system from Gamaredon.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-8088
vulnerabilities.description: A patched WinRAR vulnerability exploited by Gamaredon for payload delivery and to place downloaders in the Windows Startup folder for persistence.

identities.name: Ukrainian government and military entities
identities.description: The primary targets of Gamaredon's cyber espionage campaign in Ukraine.

infrastructure.name: Cloudflare C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Legitimate Cloudflare tunnels and serverless workers abused by Gamaredon for command-and-control to evade detection by blending in with normal web traffic.

indicators.name: Gamaredon PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass
indicators.pattern:
title: Gamaredon PowerShell Execution Policy Bypass
id: 2b8c1f3a-1e9d-4b7c-8a0d-9f6e5d4c3b2a
status: experimental
description: Monitor for PowerShell being launched with a bypass policy to run an unsigned script, a common Gamaredon TTP.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/gamaredon-expands-ukraine-attacks-with.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-29
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - ' -ExecutionPolicy Bypass'
      - ' -File'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell being launched with parameters to bypass the execution policy and run a script file, a technique frequently used by Gamaredon.

indicators.name: Gamaredon LNK/Script in Startup Folder
indicators.pattern:
title: Gamaredon LNK/Script in Startup Folder
id: 8d3e4f5a-9c6b-4a2d-8f1e-3a2b1c0d9e8f
status: experimental
description: Monitor the user Startup folder for the creation of new LNK, VBS, or PowerShell files, a persistence method used by Gamaredon.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/gamaredon-expands-ukraine-attacks-with.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-29
tags:
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: '\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup\'
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.lnk'
      - '.vbs'
      - '.ps1'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of LNK, VBS, or PS1 files in the Windows Startup folder, a persistence technique used by Gamaredon.

indicators.name: Potential Gamaredon C2 via Cloudflare Tunnel
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Gamaredon C2 via Cloudflare Tunnel
id: 5a9b8c7d-6f5e-4d3c-2b1a-0e9f8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to trycloudflare.com, which is used by Cloudflare Tunnels and has been abused by Gamaredon for C2. High potential for FPs, requires context.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/gamaredon-expands-ukraine-attacks-with.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-29
tags:
  - attack.t1102.002
  - attack.command_and_control
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith: '.trycloudflare.com'
  filter_legit:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cloudflared.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries for *.trycloudflare.com domains, which could indicate C2 communications via Cloudflare Tunnels as used by Gamaredon. Legitimate use by cloudflared.exe should be filtered.

indicators.name: Suspicious HTA or LNK Attachment Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious HTA or LNK Attachment Creation
id: 1e2f3a4b-5c6d-4e7f-8a9b-0c1d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects HTA or LNK files, commonly used by Gamaredon in spear-phishing campaigns, being created by common email or browser processes.
references:
  - https://thehackernews.com/2026/06/gamaredon-expands-ukraine-attacks-with.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-29
tags:
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.initial_access
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_file:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.hta'
      - '.lnk'
  selection_source:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects the creation of HTA or LNK files by email clients or web browsers, which is a common delivery method for Gamaredon's spear-phishing attacks.

relationships.target_name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder

relationships.source_name: Potential Gamaredon C2 via Cloudflare Tunnel
Suspicious HTA or LNK Attachment Creation
